ucs.title: [LLM] Sandworm pre-wiper recovery-destruction chain (NotPetya / CaddyWiper / HermeticWiper signature)
ucs.description: Hunts the multi-tool 'scorched earth' precursor that Sandworm (GRU 74455 / Seashell Blizzard / Iron Viking) runs minutes before detonating its wipers and pseudo-ransomware (NotPetya, Olympic Destroyer, KillDisk, CaddyWiper, HermeticWiper, IsaacWiper, AcidRain, Industroyer2). The actor characteristically chains several recovery-destruction native binaries on the same host inside the same hour — vssadmin/wmic shadow-copy delete, wbadmin catalog delete, bcdedit recovery-disable, wevtutil log clear, fsutil USN-journal delete — to ensure rollback is impossible. Two or more of these from one host/ac
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as tools dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_destructive_tools min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("vssadmin.exe","wbadmin.exe","wmic.exe","bcdedit.exe","wevtutil.exe","fsutil.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND ( (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*shadows*") OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*catalog*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND (Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*No*" OR Processes.process="*bootstatuspolicy*ignoreallfailures*")) OR (Processes.process_name="wevtutil.exe" AND (Processes.process="*cl *" OR Processes.process="*clear-log*")) OR (Processes.process_name="fsutil.exe" AND Processes.process="*usn*deletejournal*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (Processes.process="*Win32_Shadowcopy*" OR Processes.process="*Remove-WmiObject*Shadowcopy*")) ) by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=1h | where distinct_destructive_tools >= 2 AND NOT match(Processes.user, "\$$") | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Sandworm pre-wiper recovery-destruction chain — ≥2 distinct destructive native tools on one host/user/hour
let WipeBins = dynamic(["vssadmin.exe","wbadmin.exe","wmic.exe","bcdedit.exe","wevtutil.exe","fsutil.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                                  // exclude machine accounts
| where FileName in~ (WipeBins)
| extend Cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe"  and Cmd has_all ("delete","shadows"))
     or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe"   and Cmd has_all ("delete","catalog"))
     or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe"      and Cmd has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
     or (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"   and (Cmd has "recoveryenabled no" or Cmd has "bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures"))
     or (FileName =~ "wevtutil.exe"  and (Cmd has " cl " or Cmd has "clear-log"))
     or (FileName =~ "fsutil.exe"    and Cmd has_all ("usn","deletejournal"))
     or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
         and (Cmd has "win32_shadowcopy" or Cmd has "remove-wmiobject" and Cmd has "shadowcopy"))
| summarize DistinctTools  = dcount(FileName),
            Tools          = make_set(FileName, 10),
            Cmdlines       = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10),
            ParentImages   = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
            FirstSeen      = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen       = max(Timestamp)
    by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where DistinctTools >= 2                                          // chain, not a one-off admin task
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sandworm pre-wiper recovery-destruction chain — Sentinel SecurityEvent EID 4688
let WipeBins = dynamic(["vssadmin.exe","wbadmin.exe","wmic.exe","bcdedit.exe","wevtutil.exe","fsutil.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         Cmd     = tolower(CommandLine)
| where BinName in (WipeBins)
| where (BinName == "vssadmin.exe"  and Cmd has_all ("delete","shadows"))
     or (BinName == "wbadmin.exe"   and Cmd has_all ("delete","catalog"))
     or (BinName == "wmic.exe"      and Cmd has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
     or (BinName == "bcdedit.exe"   and (Cmd has "recoveryenabled no" or Cmd has "bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures"))
     or (BinName == "wevtutil.exe"  and (Cmd has " cl " or Cmd has "clear-log"))
     or (BinName == "fsutil.exe"    and Cmd has_all ("usn","deletejournal"))
     or (BinName in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
         and (Cmd has "win32_shadowcopy" or (Cmd has "remove-wmiobject" and Cmd has "shadowcopy")))
| summarize DistinctTools = dcount(BinName),
            Tools         = make_set(BinName, 10),
            Cmdlines      = make_set(CommandLine, 10),
            ParentImages  = make_set(ParentProcessName, 10),
            FirstSeen     = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen      = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where DistinctTools >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Single destructive native commands (vssadmin delete shadows on its own) are noisy and frequently match legitimate maintenance — but Sandworm's reusable destructive playbook (publicly documented across NotPetya, Olympic Destroyer, KillDisk-Ukraine, CaddyWiper, HermeticWiper and Industroyer2 by CISA AA22-057A, Mandiant, ESET and Microsoft Threat Intelligence) deterministically chains 3–5 of these bi

ucs.title: [LLM] Sandworm WMIC remote-process fan-out (NotPetya/Olympic Destroyer cross-host wiper deployment)
ucs.description: Hunts Sandworm's signature WMI-based lateral self-propagation: a single host invoking `wmic.exe /node:<target> process call create` against three or more remote hosts inside a 30-minute window. This is the technique NotPetya (2017), KillDisk-Ukraine, and Olympic Destroyer used to detonate the wiper enterprise-wide after stealing creds with mimikatz/LSASS dumping (T1003.001) — and that the GRU continues to use against Ukrainian targets per CERT-UA reporting on Industroyer2 / CaddyWiper deployment.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*/node:*" AND Processes.process="*process*" AND Processes.process="*call*" AND Processes.process="*create*" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process _time span=30m | rex field=cmdlines "(?i)/node:\"?(?<target_node>[^\s\"]+)" max_match=0 | stats count dc(target_node) as target_count values(target_node) as targets values(cmdlines) as cmdlines values(parents) as parents min(first_seen) as first_seen max(last_seen) as last_seen by dest user | where target_count >= 3 AND NOT match(user, "\$$") | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Sandworm WMIC fan-out — one host launching wmic /node: process call create against ≥3 remote nodes/30m
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "wmic.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/node:"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_all ("process","call","create")
| extend TargetNode = tolower(extract(@"(?i)/node:""?([^\s""]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine))
| where isnotempty(TargetNode) and TargetNode !in ("localhost",".","127.0.0.1")
| summarize TargetCount  = dcount(TargetNode),
            Targets      = make_set(TargetNode, 50),
            Cmdlines     = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10),
            ParentImage  = any(InitiatingProcessFileName),
            ParentCmd    = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            FirstSeen    = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen     = max(Timestamp)
    by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, 30m)
| where TargetCount >= 3                                                  // 3 = empirical fan-out floor; admin scripts rarely cross 2 in 30m
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sandworm WMIC fan-out — Sentinel SecurityEvent EID 4688 (requires command-line auditing GPO)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User" and Account !endswith "$"
| extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where BinName == "wmic.exe"
| where CommandLine has "/node:"
| where CommandLine has_all ("process","call","create")
| extend TargetNode = tolower(extract(@"(?i)/node:""?([^\s""]+)", 1, CommandLine))
| where isnotempty(TargetNode) and TargetNode !in ("localhost",".","127.0.0.1")
| summarize TargetCount  = dcount(TargetNode),
            Targets      = make_set(TargetNode, 50),
            Cmdlines     = make_set(CommandLine, 10),
            ParentImage  = any(ParentProcessName),
            FirstSeen    = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen     = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, 30m)
| where TargetCount >= 3
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Sandworm WMIC Remote Process Fan-Out (NotPetya / Olympic Destroyer style)
id: 7d2a5f6c-9b41-4e7e-bd0e-sandworm-wmic-fanout
status: experimental
description: Detects wmic.exe spawning remote processes on other hosts via /node:<target> process call create. Sandworm (G0034 / Seashell Blizzard) characteristically uses this technique to fan out wipers and pseudo-ransomware after credential theft.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0034/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa22-057a
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/sandworm-disrupts-power-ukraine-operational-technology
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1047
  - attack.lateral_movement
  - attack.t1021.002
  - detection.threat_hunting
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
  selection_args_all:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '/node:'
      - 'process'
      - 'call'
      - 'create'
  filter_local:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/node:localhost'
      - '/node:.'
      - '/node:127.0.0.1'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_img and selection_args_all and not (filter_local or filter_machine_acct)
falsepositives:
  - Centralised admin tooling that legitimately fans WMI commands to many hosts (SCCM, Tanium, custom scripts) — baseline and exclude the source host/account.
level: high
ucs.rationale: Sandworm (CrowdStrike Voodoo Bear / Microsoft Seashell Blizzard / Mandiant UNC2589 / Iron Viking) operates GRU Unit 74455 and is the only nation-state actor with a public, documented track record of using WMIC /node fan-out as the deployment mechanism for destructive payloads at enterprise scale: NotPetya (2017), Olympic Destroyer (2018), and the 2022–2023 Ukraine wave of CaddyWiper / HermeticWipe
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
